orderedActions.title: Feed founder feedback directly into ranking and escalation policy
orderedActions.rationale: Founder feedback now carries 37.5% linkage and 38% escalation pressure; ranking should respond to that behavior directly.
orderedActions.firstStep: Map explicit approve/defer/reject/edit feedback signals into recommendation weighting and queue escalation posture.
orderedActions.successMetric: Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Map explicit approve/defer/reject/edit feedback signals into recommendation weig...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Core assumptions appear well-grounded given current evidence depth and uncertainty.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 1 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it.

orderedActions.title: Turn leverage modeling into a regime-aware studio allocation engine
orderedActions.rationale: Leverage scoring is useful, but studio-wide decisions require a recommended split of founder, engineering, and creative attention tied to regime and dependency pressure.
orderedActions.firstStep: Attach explicit founder/engineering/creative allocation recommendations to the top leverage targets and summarize the best current studio-wide allocation move.
orderedActions.successMetric: Portfolio leverage outputs name both the project and the recommended time split for acting on it.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Portfolio leverage outputs name both the project and the recommended time split for acting on it.

orderedActions.title: Push dashboard and hub summaries onto the operator rendering spine
orderedActions.rationale: Startup already inherits operator posture; dashboard and hub views should stop maintaining parallel summary logic before drift reappears.
orderedActions.firstStep: Route dashboard and hub-facing summaries through the operator command center so posture, trust movement, and unlock status share one runtime source.
orderedActions.successMetric: Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Route dashboard and hub-facing summaries through the operator command center so ...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Core assumptions appear well-grounded given current evidence depth and uncertainty.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 2 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture.

orderedActions.title: Expand evidence provenance and contradiction tracing
orderedActions.rationale: Evidence density is 3.55 with 0% stale nodes; provenance and contradiction edges should drive trust explicitly.
orderedActions.firstStep: Persist provenance metadata, contradiction edges, and citation-like source links in the evidence graph.
orderedActions.successMetric: Every major recommendation can show evidence depth, freshness, and contradiction pressure.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Every major recommendation can show evidence depth, freshness, and contradiction pressure.

orderedActions.title: Harden shared external feed validation
orderedActions.rationale: The external feed seam now carries decision-grade adapters, so manifest-level validation and downgrade rules need to be stricter than generic feed-valid/feed-invalid tagging.
orderedActions.firstStep: Add adapter manifests, required-signal validation, and invalid-feed downgrade receipts for each high-risk external adapter.
orderedActions.successMetric: Signal coverage receipts explain why an adapter is trusted, invalid, or still shadow-only instead of showing generic feed status only.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Signal coverage receipts explain why an adapter is trusted, invalid, or still shadow-only instead of showing generic feed status only.

orderedActions.title: Deepen outcome learning and regret tracking
orderedActions.rationale: Recommendation quality should improve from measured results, not narrative confidence; hit rate is 100% and regret is 0.25.
orderedActions.firstStep: Convert open hypotheses into intervention IDs with baselines, horizons, and later-session observations, then let regret directly demote weak strategies and actions.
orderedActions.successMetric: Top actions show improving hit rate, stronger experiment coverage, and decreasing regret over time.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions show improving hit rate, stronger experiment coverage, and decreasing regret over time.

orderedActions.title: Increase external evidence connector coverage
orderedActions.rationale: Coverage is 65%; higher autonomy is unjustified until external evidence replaces local proxies.
orderedActions.firstStep: Add real GitHub/review/deploy/proposal/founder-override evidence sources and rank them by expected decision lift.
orderedActions.successMetric: Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Add real GitHub/review/deploy/proposal/founder-override evidence sources and ran...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Assumes 69% evidence depth is sufficient to act; a deeper evidence pass may reverse this recommendation.
preConditions: Significant effort (50%) required — confirm scope is achievable before approving.
Verify success metric: "Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 1 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs.

orderedActions.title: Grade top recommendations against do, delay, and opposite counterfactuals
orderedActions.rationale: Recommendation authority remains soft until IGNIS compares the selected path against plausible alternatives and resolves those differences over time.
orderedActions.firstStep: Attach compact do/delay/opposite scenarios to the top actions so later outcomes can grade whether the chosen path was actually superior.
orderedActions.successMetric: Top actions carry explicit alternative-path expectations that can be checked in later sessions.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions carry explicit alternative-path expectations that can be checked in later sessions.

sequenceRationale: 1. Feed founder feedback directly into ranking and escalation policy
2. Turn leverage modeling into a regime-aware studio allocation engine
3. Push dashboard and hub summaries onto the operator rendering spine
4. Expand evidence provenance and contradiction tracing
6. Deepen outcome learning and regret tracking
8. Grade top recommendations against do, delay, and opposite counterfactuals
